instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `with-saved-vars` that saves the current values of a list of variables into hidden gensyms, runs body, and restores them on exit, even if a non-local control transfer occurs.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro with-saved-vars (vars &body body)
  (let ((saves (loop for v in vars collect (gensym))))
    `(let ,(mapcar #'list saves vars)
       (unwind-protect (progn ,@body)
         ,@(mapcar (lambda (v s) `(setf ,v ,s)) vars saves)))))
